scope: phase-1 post-landing audit — catalog only, no refactors performed
summary: Phase 1 code quality is broadly good: modular crate boundaries are respected, no deep-nesting hotspots, zero unsafe code. The primary refactoring debt is concentrated in one oversized file (benten-engine/src/lib.rs at 3,151 LOC which is a 1.76x growth against the G7 code-reviewer's 1,789 baseline), a uniform stale/budget pattern replicated across 5 IVM views, 4 copies of a fresh_engine() test helper, a pervasive 'unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())' poisoned-mutex idiom (35 occurrences) that could be a single helper, 3 test-only pub methods leaking through the public API (g7-cr-4 residual), and the STUB_MARKER scaffolding in three stub-phase crates. 14 live TODO(R4b) / TODO(G8) / TODO(phase-1-cleanup) markers should be escalated to Phase 2 issue-tracking now that Phase 1 is closed. No action should block R4b / R6 quality council; all items are mechanical clean-ups or defer-to-Phase-2 escalations.

benten-engine.assessment: under-modularized — single lib.rs holds Engine+Builder+ChangeProbe+SubgraphSpec+PrimitiveHost impl+transaction+10 trait impls; natural split into ~6 sub-modules available

benten-ivm.assessment: healthy; views/ should be mod.rs-exposed (see F-nit-01)

findings.claim: Single file grew from G7 baseline of 1,789 LOC to 3,151 LOC (+76%) across G7 mini-review fixes, G8 landing, and pass-2 triage. The module now owns: EngineError+static_for(51 LOC); EngineInner+drain (42 LOC); Engine inherent (CRUD, register, call, dispatch_call, trace, mermaid, version stubs — 1,200+ LOC); PrimitiveHost impl (170 LOC); EngineBuilder + assemble (220 LOC); BackendGrantReader (95 LOC); outcome_from_terminal + resolve_list_via_view_or_backend (140 LOC); Outcome, Trace, TraceStep, ChangeProbe (250 LOC); EngineTransaction + NestedTx + GraphTxLike (80 LOC); SubgraphSpec + SubgraphSpecBuilder + IterateBody + WriteSpec (280 LOC); IntoSubgraphSpec / GrantSubject / RevokeSubject / RevokeScope / IntoCallInput trait adapters (270 LOC); testing module (120 LOC); inline tests (~90 LOC).
findings.impact: New engineers cannot ctrl-F to the responsible surface. Mini-review diffs on any one area force context load of the whole file. Diff-review fatigue measurably slows R5/R6 cycles.
findings.suggested_refactor: Split into: src/error.rs (EngineError+static_for), src/builder.rs (EngineBuilder+BackendGrantReader+assemble), src/dispatch.rs (dispatch_call+dispatch_call_inner+subgraph_for_crud+subgraph_for_spec+lookup_cid_by_base32+outcome mapping), src/primitive_host.rs (impl PrimitiveHost), src/subgraph_spec.rs (SubgraphSpec+Builder+WriteSpec+IterateBody+Into adapters), src/outcome.rs (Outcome+Trace+TraceStep+TerminalError), src/change_probe.rs (ChangeProbe+EngineInner events), src/transaction.rs (EngineTransaction+NestedTx+GraphTxLike), src/testing.rs (existing pub mod testing). Engine struct and open/builder/ivm wiring stay in lib.rs (~400 LOC target).
findings.effort: 3–5 hour mechanical extraction; no behavior change; all tests must stay green by construction.
findings.phase: R6 clean-up candidate OR Phase-2 first issue

findings.claim: All 5 IVM views (capability_grants, content_listing, event_handler_dispatch, governance_inheritance, version_current) re-implement the same budget+stale state machine: `remaining_budget: u64`, `original_budget: u64`, `stale: bool`, `fn with_budget_for_testing(budget)`, `fn rebuild() -> ... { clear_state; remaining_budget = original_budget; stale = false; Ok(()) }`, `fn is_stale(&self)`, `fn mark_stale(&mut self)`, and the decrement-or-flip-stale branch inside `on_change`. Grep shows 61 occurrences of remaining_budget/original_budget across the 5 files, 17 direct matches for the state-mutation methods.
findings.impact: A bug in the stale machinery must be fixed 5x. g5-cr-3 (rebuild must restore original_budget) was a single finding that required 5 identical patches. Phase 2's pattern-based view registrar cannot lift the machinery cleanly until it's de-duplicated.
findings.suggested_refactor: Introduce `struct ViewBudget { remaining: u64, original: u64, stale: bool }` with methods `new(budget)`, `consume() -> Result<(), ViewError::Stale>`, `reset()`. Each view holds `budget: ViewBudget` as its first field; the 5 `rebuild` / `is_stale` / `mark_stale` bodies collapse to 1-line delegations. Estimated 250 LOC reduction across views/*.
findings.effort: 2 hour extraction + re-run existing view tests (no new tests required — the machinery is already exhaustively tested per view).

findings.location: crates/benten-engine/src/lib.rs (26 occurrences), crates/benten-graph/src/* (4 occurrences), crates/benten-engine/src/change.rs (3 occurrences) — 35 total cross-workspace
findings.claim: The `.lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner())` poisoned-mutex recovery idiom is duplicated 35 times. Every `std::sync::Mutex::lock()` call site pays 3 lines of defensive boilerplate.
findings.impact: Readability: the idiom drowns the business logic; ctrl-F for `.lock()` highlights noise. If the crate ever chooses a different recovery discipline (e.g. propagate-panic, or a parking_lot switch), all 35 sites must be touched.
findings.suggested_refactor: Add `pub(crate) trait MutexExt<T> { fn lock_poison_ok(&self) -> MutexGuard<'_, T>; }` with a blanket impl at benten-engine crate root. Call sites become `self.inner.handlers.lock_poison_ok()`. For cross-crate reuse, live in benten-core behind a `sync` module.
findings.effort: 1 hour trait introduction + 35-site replace (sed-able since the idiom is bit-for-bit identical).

findings.claim: `fn fresh_engine() -> (tempfile::TempDir, Engine)` is copy-pasted across 4 test files with two slightly different tuple orderings ((Engine, TempDir) vs (TempDir, Engine)). The bodies are bit-for-bit identical: `let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap(); let engine = Engine::builder().path(dir.path().join("benten.redb")).build().unwrap(); (dir, engine)`.
findings.impact: Four copies drift if the canonical builder defaults shift; already produced two tuple-order variants which force readers to recheck destructuring at every call site.
findings.suggested_refactor: Lift to `tests/support.rs` (or `tests/common/mod.rs` under the workspace-convention for shared integration helpers). Canonicalize the return tuple to (TempDir, Engine). Same with the also-duplicated `fn post(title)` / `fn post_node(title, body)` / `fn make_post(title, created_at)` helpers (7 copies across crates/benten-engine/tests/, crates/benten-graph/tests/, crates/benten-ivm/tests/).

findings.claim: `tempfile::tempdir().unwrap()` appears 105 times across 55 files with no common helper; most immediately followed by `.path().join("benten.redb")`. At least 30 sites additionally wrap into an `Engine::builder()...build().unwrap()` chain.
findings.impact: Testing-path boilerplate is the second-most-common copy-paste across crates. Makes search-for-real-logic noisy and guarantees any future change to the redb filename / builder default is a 30+-site touch.
findings.suggested_refactor: Add `benten-engine/tests/support.rs` with `fn ephemeral_engine() -> (TempDir, Engine)` and `fn ephemeral_redb_path() -> (TempDir, PathBuf)`. Keep the bare `tempdir()` call for one-off tests that need unusual setup.
findings.effort: Same clean-up as R-minor-01; combine into one PR.

findings.claim: Three test-only methods are exposed as `pub` without gate: `SubgraphBuilder::build_unvalidated_for_test`, `SubgraphBuilder::force_add_cross_edges_for_testing`, `SubgraphBuilder::build_validated_for_corruption_test`. g7-cr-4 flagged these; g8 did not act. They are reachable from `cargo doc` as part of the public API surface.
findings.impact: A downstream DSL consumer who types `SubgraphBuilder::` and hits autocomplete will see _for_test / _for_testing methods alongside legitimate builder methods. The `#[doc(hidden)]` attribute is not set. `build_unvalidated_for_test` is additionally called from benten-engine/src/lib.rs:988/1010/1023/1063/1131/2880 — a legitimate non-test cross-crate call — which means it is NOT purely test-only and the name lies about it.
findings.suggested_refactor: Option A (conservative): add `#[doc(hidden)]` to all three, rename `build_unvalidated_for_test` → `build_unvalidated` (since benten-engine relies on it for its dispatch shape construction) with a doc comment pinning the purpose. Option B (aggressive): move the force_add_cross_edges / build_validated_for_corruption_test methods behind a `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-helpers"))]` gate since they have NO production call sites; leave build_unvalidated_for_test public and rename it.
findings.effort: Option A: 15 minutes. Option B: 30 minutes + workspace Cargo.toml feature plumbing.

findings.claim: Three engine methods already use `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-helpers"))]` correctly: `test_subscribe_all_change_events`, `test_subscribe_change_events_matching_label`, `testing_insert_privileged_fixture`. Good. BUT `Engine::call_with_revocation_at` (line 646), `EngineBuilder::with_policy_allowing_revocation` (1693), `EngineBuilder::with_test_ivm_budget` (1724), and the `WriteSpec::test_inject_failure` method (2812) are publicly exposed without gates even though their only callers are tests + `call_with_revocation_at` currently returns Phase-1 no-ops. The drift here mirrors R-minor-03.
findings.impact: `test_inject_failure` is observable in the Phase-1 public DSL — a real consumer can corrupt their handler by passing `true` and the outcome routes through ON_ERROR with fabricated error text. That is a footgun, not a capability.
findings.suggested_refactor: Gate `WriteSpec::test_inject_failure`, `EngineBuilder::with_test_ivm_budget`, and `EngineBuilder::with_policy_allowing_revocation` behind `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-helpers"))]` OR rename with a `.unstable_` prefix and document as Phase-2 provisional.
findings.effort: 30 minutes + enable the feature in dev-dependencies for the dependent test crates.

findings.claim: Two public `pub fn read_view_strict` / `pub fn read_view_allow_stale` methods on Engine are identical to `read_view` and `read_view_with(_, ReadViewOptions::allow_stale())`. They add no capability beyond the canonical `read_view_with` path. Zero doc comment; low-quality pub surface.
findings.impact: Drift: Phase-2 semantics changes to `read_view` must also remember the two aliases. Autocomplete clutter.
findings.suggested_refactor: Either delete both (callers use `read_view_with`) or document each with a one-sentence 'convenience wrapper' doc comment. Grep confirms `read_view_strict` has 1 test call site and `read_view_allow_stale` has 0.

findings.claim: 9 live TODO(R4b) / TODO(R4b / G7) / TODO(R4b / Phase-2) markers remain in production code after R4b gate-close. Each references a specific mini-review finding (g3-ce-5, g5-cr-1, g5-ivm-12/13/14, g6-cr-8) but has NO issue-tracker ticket or conventional TODO id suffix. R5-G5-pass2-ivm-algorithm-b-reviewer finding g5-pass2-2 already flagged this (severity minor) and recommended renaming to `TODO(R4b-cascade)` / `TODO(R4b-rebuild-equiv)` etc.
findings.impact: A Phase 2 dispatcher scanning for work has no canonical index. Future agents parse 9 free-form TODOs in 8 files with no grouping.
findings.suggested_refactor: Decision point for Ben: (a) convert every TODO(R4b) to a tracked GitHub issue and leave a `TODO(issue-N)` reference, or (b) rename all to `TODO(phase-2-<tag>)` matching the existing TODO(phase-2-*) convention (18 uses, better-anchored). Option (b) is lower-cost and matches prior art.
findings.effort: 1 hour rename + 1-line crate-doc comment per affected file pointing at .addl/phase-1/r5-*.json.
findings.phase: R6 clean-up candidate (escalate before Phase-2 dispatch)

findings.claim: 3 TODO(G8) / TODO(G6) / TODO(phase-2-grant-backed-policy) markers live in code paths where the related gate has already closed. `TODO(G6): honor wall-clock bound in addition to iteration count per R4b compromise #1 tightening (auditor finding g4-p2-uc-2)` — G6 is closed. `TODO(G8): napi v3 cannot serialize Box<dyn CapabilityPolicy>` — G8 is closed; the PolicyKind enum was NOT added and the limitation is permanent for Phase 1.
findings.impact: Group labels (G6, G8) in comments mislead future readers: they imply the work is still pending within Phase 1 when it's actually been deferred to Phase 2. Semantically the TODOs are correct; syntactically they need re-labeling.
findings.suggested_refactor: Rename TODO(G6) → TODO(phase-2-wall-clock-cap-bound), TODO(G8) → TODO(phase-2-policy-kind-enum), and cross-link in .addl/phase-1/r5-g7-mini-code-reviewer.json / r4b-qa-expert.json.

findings.claim: TODO(phase-1-cleanup, G8) markers for retiring the 3 `STUB_MARKER` constants and the 3 `const _: &str = benten_*::STUB_MARKER` assertions in benten-engine/src/lib.rs:47-49. G8 has landed; the crates are no longer stub-phase in practice; the markers + assertions are dead.
findings.impact: Documentation lies: readers see 'STUB' in three crate names long past the stub era and infer the crates are not real.
findings.suggested_refactor: Delete all 3 `pub const STUB_MARKER` definitions in benten-caps/benten-eval/benten-ivm and the 3 matching `const _: &str = ...::STUB_MARKER;` reference lines in benten-engine/src/lib.rs:47-49. One-commit deletion.
findings.phase: R6 clean-up candidate (specifically named as phase-1-cleanup — ship now)

findings.claim: `ActiveCall` struct has 3 `#[allow(dead_code, reason = "retained for future capability-binding uses")]` fields: handler_id, op, actor. None are read at any call site. They exist 'for Phase-2 capability-binding'.
findings.impact: Dead fields masked by allow(dead_code) is exactly the 'rationalized dead code' the user calls out as laziness. If retained for Phase 2, they should be a Phase-2 task on an issue; if speculatively kept, they should be removed and re-added when needed.
findings.suggested_refactor: Delete the three fields; re-add in Phase 2 when the capability-binding wire-through lands (it will require new code at both ends of the thread — trivial to thread when the Phase-2 code lands). OR: keep but replace the allow attribute with an explicit `TODO(phase-2-actor-threading)` comment referencing the R1 attack-vector #9.

findings.claim: `EngineTransaction::create_node` is documented as 'Alias for `Self::put_node`' with a one-line body. This is the only `create_*` method on `EngineTransaction`; the inherent `Engine::create_node` / `Engine::create_edge` are the canonical create/delete surface. Having `create_node` as an alias for `put_node` on one type but the canonical name on another type is a 3-minute confusion for every new reader.
findings.impact: Drift between the inherent Engine (create_node canonical) and EngineTransaction (create_node = alias for put_node). Encourages cargo-cult create_* usage.
findings.suggested_refactor: Decide: either delete `EngineTransaction::create_node` alias (force `put_node` inside transactions, matching redb vocabulary) OR rename `Engine::create_node` to `Engine::put_node` (breaking). Option A is safer and matches the 'put' mental model already used by the Transaction trait. Option A deprecates the 2 test call sites of `EngineTransaction::create_node`.

findings.claim: `has_unrevoked_grant_for_scope` and `revoked_scopes` both iterate label-indexed CIDs, both call `backend.get_node(&cid)` inside a match, both wrap `GraphError` into `CapError::Denied { required: format!("backend read: {e:?}"), entity: String::new() }`. The `backend read: {e:?}` lambda is copy-pasted 4 times within 50 lines. `CapError::Denied` is being used for read failure — semantically `CapError::Denied` is 'permission denied', not 'backend read failure'.
findings.impact: Type-punning: a real permission denial is now indistinguishable from a redb I/O error. Downstream consumers treating CapError::Denied as permission-only will silently miss backend failures.
findings.suggested_refactor: Introduce `CapError::BackendRead { source: Box<dyn std::error::Error + Send + Sync> }` variant OR promote to `EngineError::Graph`-wrapped return at this layer. Also factor the 3 occurrences of the `map_err` lambda into a `fn wrap_backend_err(e: GraphError) -> CapError`.

findings.claim: The benten-ivm crate uses `#[path = "views/capability_grants.rs"]` attribute-based view composition in lib.rs rather than a proper `src/views/mod.rs`. TODO(R4b) at lib.rs:62-63 documents this as a 'cosmetic' cleanup. g5-cr-1 flagged it originally.
findings.impact: Unconventional module composition; new readers searching for `mod views` in lib.rs find attribute-based path directives. Does not break anything; confusing.
findings.suggested_refactor: Create `crates/benten-ivm/src/views/mod.rs` declaring `pub mod capability_grants; pub mod content_listing; ...` and replace the attribute-based declarations. Near-zero diff beyond that file.

findings.location: crates/benten-caps/src/lib.rs (grant_backed.rs, grant.rs both at crate root)
findings.claim: benten-caps has 8 top-level modules: attenuation, error, grant, grant_backed, lib, noauth, policy, ucan_stub. Module count is still healthy, but the grant/grant_backed pair implies a natural `grant/` sub-module (grant/types.rs + grant/backed.rs) if Phase 2 adds more grant-related code.
findings.impact: Zero current impact; pre-emptive flag so a Phase-2 GrantPolicy expansion does not accumulate 4 top-level `grant_*` files.
findings.suggested_refactor: Defer — signal only. If Phase-2 adds grant_delegation / grant_revocation, introduce a `grant/` subdir. Do not restructure today.

findings.claim: All 4 PrimitiveHost write methods share the same 7-line active_call guard pattern: `let mut guard = self.active_call.lock().unwrap_or_else(|e| e.into_inner()); if let Some(frame) = guard.last_mut() { frame.pending_ops.push(...); Ok(projected) } else { drop(guard); self.backend.<method>(...).map_err(|e| EvalError::Backend(format!("{e:?}"))) }`. The branch logic varies only in the variant constructor + fallback backend method.
findings.impact: 4 copies drift; adding a 5th op (PutEdge variant in pending is dead today, fills in Phase-2) requires copying the same pattern.
findings.suggested_refactor: Extract `fn defer_or_direct<F, R>(&self, push: impl FnOnce(&mut ActiveCall), fallback: F) -> Result<R, EvalError>` helper. Each PrimitiveHost method collapses to 3 lines.
findings.phase: Paired with R-major-01 engine lib split OR Phase-2

findings.claim: `register_subgraph` and `register_subgraph_aggregate` are 80% identical: same stored_spec capture, same handler-idempotency check, same specs-insert. Only the validation mode differs (strict vs aggregate). The second method should call the first with a flag OR both should call a private helper.
findings.impact: Two methods drift; aggregate path has already acquired a g6-cr-7 tweak the non-aggregate path did not.
findings.suggested_refactor: Unify: `pub fn register_subgraph<S>(&self, spec: S) -> Result<String, _>` and `pub fn register_subgraph_aggregate<S>(&self, spec: S) -> Result<String, _>` both delegate to `fn register_subgraph_with_mode(&self, spec: S, aggregate: bool) -> Result<String, _>`.
findings.phase: Paired with R-major-01 engine lib split

findings.claim: 75+ dispatch functions in one file. Each category (arith_*, str_*, arr_*, obj_*, coerce_*, fmt_*) is cohesive internally but the six categories are independent.
findings.impact: Navigation: a reader looking for `fn arr_flatten` scrolls past 600 LOC of arithmetic. Low impact given 1 file per category matches the dispatch table structure already.
findings.suggested_refactor: Split into `expr/builtins/{arith.rs,string.rs,array.rs,object.rs,coerce.rs,format.rs}`. `dispatch_builtin` calls `arith::arith_abs(args)` etc.

findings.claim: `fn static_for(c: &ErrorCode) -> &'static str` is a 38-entry match that duplicates `benten_core::ErrorCode::as_str` but downcasts to `&'static str`. The doc comment acknowledges the 'ErrorCode::Unknown' case degrades to `"E_UNKNOWN"` because we cannot promote `String → 'static`.
findings.impact: Maintenance: adding a new ErrorCode variant requires updating both `ErrorCode::as_str` AND `static_for`. The two are now 38 entries each and diverging is a silent contract violation.
findings.suggested_refactor: Push `static_str()` into `benten_core::ErrorCode` as an inherent method returning `Option<&'static str>` (None for Unknown). benten-engine's `static_for` becomes `c.static_str().unwrap_or("E_UNKNOWN")` — 1 line. Single source of truth at the catalog level.
findings.effort: 1 hour (one-time benten-core addition + collapse of static_for).

findings.location: crates/benten-eval/src/lib.rs:920-927 (Evaluator::default), multiple crates with trivial Default impls
findings.claim: Several `impl Default for <Builder>` bodies are pure delegations to `Self::new()`. Ubiquitous Rust pattern — not strictly wrong, just a reminder that every builder/ctor type in the engine+caps+eval crates has a 4-line `impl Default` that could be `#[derive(Default)]` if the inner types support it.
findings.impact: Nil; noise-level nit. Listed for completeness.
findings.suggested_refactor: None suggested. Retained shape.

findings.claim: Three stub types use the `_placeholder: ()` idiom to reserve future fields: `AnchorHandle`, `HandlerPredecessors`, `NestedTx`. The first two return NotImplemented from every Engine method; the third is constructed only inside a NotImplemented error return. All three are serialized as public types in the engine's public API.
findings.impact: Public API cruft — a consumer can construct and pass an `AnchorHandle` expecting it to be useful, but every Engine method that takes one errors. The `pub fn predecessors_of` method on HandlerPredecessors returns `&[]` unconditionally.
findings.suggested_refactor: Keep the Phase-1 NotImplemented error paths for external API stability, but replace `_placeholder: ()` with `()` newtype (`pub struct AnchorHandle(());`) so consumers cannot accidentally construct them outside the engine. Add `#[doc(hidden)]` on `predecessors_of` until it returns real data.

duplicate_test_assertions_observed: budget-trips-on-N+1 assertion replicated in each of 5 view test modules (view1..view5) AND in ivm/tests/stale_on_budget_exceeded.rs
rebuild-restores-original-budget pattern replicated 5x per g5-cr-3 patch (see R-major-02)
Node::new(vec!["post".into()], props) replicated ~60x across tests — candidate for `fn ephemeral_post(title)` in shared support.rs
do_not_refactor_now: crates/benten-eval/src/expr/parser.rs (1295 LOC) — hand-rolled Pratt parser is intentionally one unit; splitting the lexer out would fragment the byte-offset error reporting. See doc-comment at top of file.
crates/benten-graph/src/redb_backend.rs (1147 LOC) — tight coupling to redb table definitions + index maintenance makes per-method extraction costlier than the readability gain.

crates/benten-eval/src/invariants.rs (691 LOC) — eight invariant checks in one validate_subgraph function is correct; aggregate-mode bookkeeping requires a shared RegistrationError accumulator that's easier to read in one function than threaded through 8 helpers.
recommended_action_order_if_ben_dispatch: 1. R-minor-08 (delete STUB_MARKER + assertions; 15 min; labelled phase-1-cleanup)
2. R-minor-06 / R-minor-07 (TODO(R4b) + TODO(G6/G8) renames; 1 hour; escalation prep for Phase-2)
3. R-major-02 (IVM ViewBudget extraction; 2 hours; highest payoff / code-deletion ratio)
4. R-minor-01 + R-minor-02 + R-minor-04 (test helpers + test-only pub gates; 2 hours; single PR)
5. R-major-03 (MutexExt trait for lock_poison_ok; 1 hour; 35-site mechanical replace)
6. R-major-01 (benten-engine lib.rs split; 3-5 hours; biggest win for future legibility, also highest risk of touching many files)
Items 1-2 are Phase-1 housekeeping (should ship with R4b-close). Items 3-5 are R6 clean-up candidates. Item 6 is a dedicated PR — likely Phase-2 first or best after quality-council gate.

orchestrator_guidance.total_r6_clean_up_hours: ~8 hours across 10 items if all accepted; ~2 hours for the ship-with-r4b-close subset
